title: grants — error catalogue
description: Error codes this module emits. `user_facing` controls whether the message renders to end users (translated via i18n). `retryable` signals client-side retry. `audit_severity` routes to the correct log channel — `alert` pages on-call, `critical` fires the security-incident notification pipeline.

errors.description: Attempt to update / revoke a grant that is already revoked or expired.

Attempt to PATCH `subject_id / resource_type / resource_id / permissions / decision / granted_by / granted_at / source / source_reference / tenant_id`. Only expires_at + reason + metadata are patchable post-issue.
Tenant-isolation breach attempt: subject.tenant_id !== resource.tenant_id !== grantor.tenant_id. Fires GrantCrossTenantAttempt event pre-refusal. Security-critical.
Grantor tried to grant permissions they do not hold. Fires SelfServiceGrantAttempted event pre-refusal. Security-critical.
One or more permission names in the array don't resolve to rows in the access/rbac permissions table.
resource_type is not in GrantableRegistry. Almost always indicates a missing #[Grantable] attribute + registration in the consumer module's service provider boot.
subject_type not in the registered subject-type list. Wave 1b: only `user` is supported.
resource_id does not resolve to a row of resource_type.
subject_id does not resolve to a row of subject_type in the caller's tenant.
reason < 20 characters (or configured settings.grants.min_reason_length).
reason matches a placeholder pattern (`test`, `temp`, `TODO`, `because`, single word without whitespace). Enforces the audit-trail promise: every grant has a meaningful justification.
expires_at, when supplied, must be > now().
expires_at exceeds settings.grants.expiration.max_ttl_days (default 365). Enterprise tenants with `permanent_grants` entitlement can bypass by passing NULL.
expires_at IS NULL requires the `permanent_grants` entitlement (enterprise). Other tiers must set an expiry.
decision='deny' requires the `deny_grants` entitlement (enterprise). Other tiers can only grant allow.
Bulk provisioning requires the `bulk_grant_provision` entitlement (medium plan+).

Cross-role grants (grantor lacks the permission) require the `cross_role_grants` entitlement AND a matching workflow/approvals template. Refused when the approval instance is not present in the request context.
An active grant with the same (subject, resource, permissions, decision) tuple already exists. Prevents accidental duplicates. Revoke the existing one first if the intent is to reset.
Bulk CSV upload failed validation. Response body includes per-row error map so the uploader can correct + retry.
Bulk CSV exceeds settings.grants.bulk.max_rows_per_batch (default 10000). Split into smaller batches.
The auth_user was hard-deleted mid-request. Should be nearly impossible; alerts on-call.
AccessRequestApprovedListener received an ApprovalExecuted event whose context_json did not shape into a valid grant payload. Usually indicates schema drift between access/requests and grants.
